title: UI Portal Portfolio — Portfolio View, Transaction History & Statements
problem_statement: An investor can trade through the portal but cannot see the results of their activity in the UI. There is no screen to view holdings and performance, no history of transactions, and no way to access periodic statements. Without these views, investors cannot understand their position, review past activity, or retrieve records.

goals.goal: Show investors their holdings and performance.
goals.success_measure: The portfolio view displays total value, day change, each position with unrealised P&L, and a performance chart, clearly indicating data staleness via valuedAt.
goals.measurement_method: E2E asserting portfolio summary, positions with P&L, and the performance chart render from API data.

goals.success_measure: Transaction history is paginated and filterable by type and date range, with filters applied server-side so results are complete across pages.
goals.measurement_method: E2E asserting server-side type/date filtering and correct pagination.

goals.success_measure: Investors can list statements and download a statement via its retrieval endpoint.
goals.measurement_method: E2E asserting the statements list renders and a download is initiated.

personas.name: Alex
personas.goal: See holdings and performance, review transactions, and download statements.
personas.pain_point: Without portfolio, history, and statement views the investor cannot assess or document their activity.

functional_requirements.description: Implement the portfolio view showing total account value, day change, and each position with quantity, average cost, current value, and unrealised P&L / P&L percent from the portfolio API, per Sections 6.6 and 15.1.
Implement a portfolio performance chart from the snapshot-based performance series (Recharts), with a clear empty/staleness state driven by valuedAt per Section 16 AT-4.
Implement a paginated transaction history table using the pagination and sort parameters in Section 15.2.
Implement transaction filtering by type and date range as server-side query parameters so pagination is correct over the filtered set, per Section 16 FX-1.
Implement a statements page listing available statements and initiating a download via the statement retrieval endpoint (redirect to presigned URL) per Section 15.3.

accessibility: Tables and charts provide accessible labels; the transaction table is navigable by keyboard.
performance: History and portfolio views use server-side pagination and filtering to avoid loading unbounded data.
observability: The UI surfaces valuedAt so investors understand when portfolio figures were last valued.

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Portfolio view shows holdings and P&L
then: total value and day change are shown
each position shows quantity, average cost, current value, and unrealised P&L / P&L percent
a performance chart is displayed from the snapshot series
and: when no snapshots exist yet, an explicit empty state is shown instead of an error

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Transaction filtering is server-side and complete
acceptance_criteria.given: an investor with more transactions than one page
acceptance_criteria.when: they filter by type and date range
then: the results reflect the filter across all pages
pagination is correct over the filtered set

acceptance_criteria.given: an investor with multiple pages of transactions
then: each page shows the requested page size in the requested sort order

acceptance_criteria.when: they select a statement to download
then: the download is initiated via the retrieval endpoint

in_scope: Portfolio view with total value, day change, and positions with P&L
Performance chart from snapshot series with staleness/empty state
Server-side transaction filtering by type and date range

out_of_scope.rationale: Filtering must be server-side for correct pagination per Section 16 FX-1.
Only periodic account statements are in v1 scope.
Not part of the v1 initial feature set.

existing_patterns: App shell, routing, and auth client from BE-8
Server-side pagination, sorting, and filtering (Section 15.2, Section 16 FX-1)
deployment_constraints: Runs within the Nginx-served UI container in the local stack

github_repos.notes: Portfolio UI (portfolio view, transaction history, statements) in /ui. Repo is created and scaffolded by BE-11 (devops-ci-cd-foundation).

spec_documents.label: BrokerEdge Design Document — Section 6.5/6.6, Section 15.1, Section 15.2, Section 15.3, Section 16 (FX-1, AT-4)
spec_documents.notes: Authoritative source for portfolio/history/statements UX and server-side filtering.

risks.description: Performance chart and day change are empty until BE-6 valuation has produced snapshots, appearing broken to users.
risks.mitigation: Render an explicit empty/staleness state keyed on valuedAt; ensure BE-6 runs before this epic is validated end-to-end.
risks.trigger: No snapshots exist for the account at render time.
risks.contingency: Show a friendly 'awaiting first end-of-day valuation' state.

risks.description: Transaction filtering implemented client-side would show incomplete pages.
risks.mitigation: Enforce server-side filtering via query parameters per Section 16 FX-1.
risks.trigger: Filtered pages show fewer than the page size while more matches exist.
risks.contingency: Move any client-side filter logic to API query parameters.

internal.description: Core UI shell, routing, and authenticated API client.
Statements produced and retrievable via batch processing.

definition_of_done: An investor can view holdings, day change, and per-position unrealised P&L.
An investor can see a performance chart with a clear staleness/empty state.
An investor can review paginated transaction history filtered server-side by type and date.
An investor can list and download account statements.
Portfolio journeys pass in the Playwright E2E suite against the local stack.

story_outline.title: Portfolio view with positions and P&L
